15: // CreateNewAccountApi creates a new account using the Go api.
16: // Returns the id of the new account as well as the password that was generated
17: func CreateNewAccountApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, loginName string) (accountId string, password string) {
18: 	c, err := loadConfig()
19: 	require.NoError(t, err)
20: 
21: 	aClient := accounts.NewClient(client)
22: 	password, err = base62.Random(16)
23: 	require.NoError(t, err)
24: 	newAccountResult, err := aClient.Create(ctx, c.AuthMethodId,
25: 		accounts.WithPasswordAccountLoginName(loginName),
26: 		accounts.WithPasswordAccountPassword(password),
27: 	)
28: 	require.NoError(t, err)
29: 
30: 	accountId = newAccountResult.Item.Id
31: 	t.Logf("Create Account: %s", accountId)
32: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
33: 		_, err := aClient.Delete(ctx, accountId)
34: 		require.NoError(t, err)
35: 	})
36: 
37: 	return
38: }
39: 
40: // CreateNewAccountCli creates a new account using the cli.
41: // Returns the id of the new account as well as the password that was generated
42: func CreateNewAccountCli(t testing.TB, loginName string) (string, string) {
43: 	c, err := loadConfig()
44: 	require.NoError(t, err)
45: 
46: 	ctx := context.Background()
47: 	password, err := base62.Random(16)
48: 	require.NoError(t, err)
49: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
50: 		e2e.WithArgs(
51: 			"accounts", "create", "password",
52: 			"-auth-method-id", c.AuthMethodId,
53: 			"-login-name", loginName,
54: 			"-password", "env://E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD",
55: 			"-name", "e2e Account "+loginName,
56: 			"-description", "e2e Account",
57: 			"-format", "json",
58: 		),
59: 		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD", password),
60: 	)
61: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
62: 
63: 	var newAccountResult accounts.AccountCreateResult
64: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newAccountResult)
65: 	require.NoError(t, err)
66: 
67: 	newAccountId := newAccountResult.Item.Id
68: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
69: 		AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
70: 		output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
71: 			e2e.WithArgs("accounts", "delete", "-id", newAccountId),
72: 		)
73: 		require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
74: 	})
75: 
76: 	t.Logf("Created Account: %s", newAccountId)
77: 
78: 	return newAccountId, password
79: }

1: // Package boundary provides methods for commonly used boundary actions that are used in end-to-end tests.
2: package boundary
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"context"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"testing"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/authmethods"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
12: 	"github.com/kelseyhightower/envconfig"
13: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14: )
15: 
16: type config struct {
17: 	Address            string `envconfig:"BOUNDARY_ADDR" required:"true"`               // e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9200
18: 	AuthMethodId       string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_AUTH_METHOD_ID" required:"true"` // e.g. ampw_1234567890
19: 	AdminLoginName     string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_LOGIN_NAME" default:"admin"`
20: 	AdminLoginPassword string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_PASSWORD" required:"true"`
21: }
22: 
23: func loadConfig() (*config, error) {
24: 	var c config
25: 	err := envconfig.Process("", &c)
26: 	if err != nil {
27: 		return nil, err
28: 	}
29: 
30: 	return &c, err
31: }
32: 
33: // NewApiClient creates a new Api client for the specified Boundary instance and
34: // attempts to authenticate it. Returns the client.
35: func NewApiClient() (*api.Client, error) {
36: 	c, err := loadConfig()
37: 	if err != nil {
38: 		return nil, err
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	client, err := api.NewClient(&api.Config{Addr: c.Address})
42: 	if err != nil {
43: 		return nil, err
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	ctx := context.Background()
47: 	authmethodsClient := authmethods.NewClient(client)
48: 	authenticationResult, err := authmethodsClient.Authenticate(ctx, c.AuthMethodId, "login",
49: 		map[string]interface{}{
50: 			"login_name": c.AdminLoginName,
51: 			"password":   c.AdminLoginPassword,
52: 		},
53: 	)
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return nil, err
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	client.SetToken(fmt.Sprint(authenticationResult.Attributes["token"]))
59: 	return client, err
60: }
61: 
62: // AuthenticateAdminCli uses the cli to authenticate the specified Boundary instance as an admin
63: func AuthenticateAdminCli(t testing.TB) {
64: 	c, err := loadConfig()
65: 	require.NoError(t, err)
66: 
67: 	AuthenticateCli(t, c.AdminLoginName, c.AdminLoginPassword)
68: }
69: 
70: // AuthenticateCli uses the cli to authenticate the specified Boundary instance

83: 		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_BOUNDARY_PASSWORD", password),
84: 	)
85: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
86: }

// Package boundary provides methods for commonly used boundary actions that are used in end-to-end tests.
package boundary

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"testing"

	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/authmethods"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
	"github.com/kelseyhightower/envconfig"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type config struct {
	Address            string `envconfig:"BOUNDARY_ADDR" required:"true"`               // e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9200
	AuthMethodId       string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_AUTH_METHOD_ID" required:"true"` // e.g. ampw_1234567890
	AdminLoginName     string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_LOGIN_NAME" default:"admin"`
	AdminLoginPassword string `envconfig:"E2E_PASSWORD_ADMIN_PASSWORD" required:"true"`

package boundary

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"testing"

	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/credentialstores"
	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// CreateNewCredentialStoreStaticApi creates a new static credential store using the Go api.
// Returns the id of the new credential store
func CreateNewCredentialStoreStaticApi(t testing.TB, ctx context.Context, client *api.Client, projectId string) string {
	csClient := credentialstores.NewClient(client)
	newCredentialStoreResult, err := csClient.Create(ctx, "static", projectId, credentialstores.WithName("e2e Credential Store"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	newCredentialStoreId := newCredentialStoreResult.Item.Id

<content>
1: package static_test
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"encoding/json"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"testing"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/cenkalti/backoff/v4"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/roles"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/sessions"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/api/users"
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/testing/internal/e2e/boundary"
16: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
17: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
18: )
19: 
20: // TestSessionCancelUserCli uses the cli to create a new user and sets up the right permissions for
21: // the user to connect to the created target. The test also confirms that an admin can cancel the
22: // user's session.
23: func TestSessionCancelUserCli(t *testing.T) {
24: 	e2e.MaybeSkipTest(t)
25: 	c, err := loadConfig()
26: 	require.NoError(t, err)
27: 
28: 	boundary.AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
29: 	newOrgId := boundary.CreateNewOrgCli(t)
30: 	newProjectId := boundary.CreateNewProjectCli(t, newOrgId)
31: 	newHostCatalogId := boundary.CreateNewHostCatalogCli(t, newProjectId)
32: 	newHostSetId := boundary.CreateNewHostSetCli(t, newHostCatalogId)
33: 	newHostId := boundary.CreateNewHostCli(t, newHostCatalogId, c.TargetIp)
34: 	boundary.AddHostToHostSetCli(t, newHostSetId, newHostId)
35: 	newTargetId := boundary.CreateNewTargetCli(t, newProjectId, c.TargetPort)
36: 	boundary.AddHostSourceToTargetCli(t, newTargetId, newHostSetId)
37: 	acctName := "e2e-account"
38: 	newAccountId, acctPassword := boundary.CreateNewAccountCli(t, acctName)
39: 	newUserId := boundary.CreateNewUserCli(t, "global")
40: 	boundary.SetAccountToUserCli(t, newUserId, newAccountId)
41: 
42: 	// Try to connect to the target as a user without permissions
43: 	ctx := context.Background()
44: 	boundary.AuthenticateCli(t, acctName, acctPassword)
45: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
46: 		e2e.WithArgs(
47: 			"connect",
48: 			"-target-id", newTargetId,
49: 			"-format", "json",
50: 			"-exec", "/usr/bin/ssh", "--",
51: 			"-l", c.TargetSshUser,
52: 			"-i", c.TargetSshKeyPath,
53: 			"-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
54: 			"-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
55: 			"-o", "IdentitiesOnly=yes", // forces the use of the provided key
56: 			"-p", "{{boundary.port}}", // this is provided by boundary
57: 			"{{boundary.ip}}",
58: 			"hostname -i",
59: 		),
60: 	)
61: 	require.Error(t, output.Err, string(output.Stdout), string(output.Stderr))
62: 	var response e2e.CliError
63: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stderr, &response)
64: 	require.NoError(t, err)
65: 	require.Equal(t, 403, response.Status)
66: 	t.Log("Successfully received an error when connecting to target as a user without permissions")
67: 
68: 	// Create a role
69: 	boundary.AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
70: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
71: 		e2e.WithArgs(
72: 			"roles", "create",
73: 			"-scope-id", newProjectId,
74: 			"-name", "e2e Role",
75: 			"-format", "json",
76: 		),
77: 	)
78: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
79: 	var newRoleResult roles.RoleCreateResult
80: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newRoleResult)
81: 	require.NoError(t, err)
82: 	newRoleId := newRoleResult.Item.Id
83: 	t.Logf("Created Role: %s", newRoleId)
84: 
85: 	// Add grant to role
86: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
87: 		e2e.WithArgs(
88: 			"roles", "add-grants",
89: 			"-id", newRoleId,
90: 			"-grant", "id=*;type=target;actions=authorize-session",
91: 		),
92: 	)
93: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
94: 
95: 	// Add user to role
96: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
97: 		e2e.WithArgs(
98: 			"roles", "add-principals",
99: 			"-id", newRoleId,
100: 			"-principal", newUserId,
101: 		),
102: 	)
103: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
104: 
105: 	// Connect to target to create a session
106: 	ctxCancel, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
107: 	errChan := make(chan *e2e.CommandResult)
108: 	go func() {
109: 		boundary.AuthenticateCli(t, acctName, acctPassword)
110: 		t.Log("Starting session as user...")
111: 		errChan <- e2e.RunCommand(ctxCancel, "boundary",
112: 			e2e.WithArgs(
113: 				"connect",
114: 				"-target-id", newTargetId,
115: 				"-exec", "/usr/bin/ssh", "--",
116: 				"-l", c.TargetSshUser,
117: 				"-i", c.TargetSshKeyPath,
118: 				"-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
119: 				"-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
120: 				"-o", "IdentitiesOnly=yes", // forces the use of the provided key
121: 				"-p", "{{boundary.port}}", // this is provided by boundary
122: 				"{{boundary.ip}}",
123: 				"hostname -i; sleep 60",
124: 			),
125: 		)
126: 	}()
127: 	t.Cleanup(cancel)
128: 
129: 	// Get list of sessions
130: 	var session *sessions.Session
131: 	err = backoff.RetryNotify(
132: 		func() error {
133: 			output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
134: 				e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "list", "-scope-id", newProjectId, "-format", "json"),
135: 			)
136: 			if output.Err != nil {
137: 				return backoff.Permanent(errors.New(string(output.Stderr)))
138: 			}
139: 
140: 			var sessionListResult sessions.SessionListResult
141: 			err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &sessionListResult)
142: 			if err != nil {
143: 				return backoff.Permanent(err)
144: 			}
145: 
146: 			sessionCount := len(sessionListResult.Items)
147: 			if sessionCount == 0 {
148: 				return errors.New("No items are appearing in the session list")
149: 			}
150: 
151: 			t.Logf("Found %d session(s)", sessionCount)
152: 			if sessionCount != 1 {
153: 				return backoff.Permanent(errors.New("Only one session was expected to be found"))
154: 			}
155: 
156: 			session = sessionListResult.Items[0]
157: 			return nil
158: 		},
159: 		backoff.WithMaxRetries(backoff.NewConstantBackOff(3*time.Second), 5),
160: 		func(err error, td time.Duration) {
161: 			t.Logf("%s. Retrying...", err.Error())
162: 		},
163: 	)
164: 	require.NoError(t, err)
165: 	assert.Equal(t, newTargetId, session.TargetId)
166: 	assert.Equal(t, newHostId, session.HostId)
167: 	require.Equal(t, "active", session.Status)
168: 
169: 	// Cancel session
170: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
171: 		e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "cancel", "-id", session.Id),
172: 	)
173: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
174: 
175: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
176: 		e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "read", "-id", session.Id, "-format", "json"),
177: 	)
178: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
179: 	var newSessionReadResult sessions.SessionReadResult
180: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newSessionReadResult)
181: 	require.NoError(t, err)
182: 	require.Condition(t, func() bool {
183: 		return newSessionReadResult.Item.Status == "canceling" || newSessionReadResult.Item.Status == "terminated"
184: 	})
185: 
186: 	// Check output from session
187: 	select {
188: 	case output := <-errChan:
189: 		// `boundary connect` returns a 255 when cancelled
190: 		require.Equal(t, output.ExitCode, 255, string(output.Stdout), string(output.Stderr))
191: 	case <-time.After(time.Second * 5):
192: 		t.Fatal("Timed out waiting for session command to exit")
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	t.Log("Successfully cancelled session")
196: }
197: 
198: // TestCreateUserApi uses the Go api to create a new user and add some grants to the user
199: func TestCreateUserApi(t *testing.T) {
200: 	e2e.MaybeSkipTest(t)
201: 	c, err := loadConfig()
202: 	require.NoError(t, err)
203: 
204: 	client, err := boundary.NewApiClient()
205: 	require.NoError(t, err)
206: 	ctx := context.Background()
207: 
208: 	newOrgId := boundary.CreateNewOrgApi(t, ctx, client)
209: 	newProjectId := boundary.CreateNewProjectApi(t, ctx, client, newOrgId)
210: 	newHostCatalogId := boundary.CreateNewHostCatalogApi(t, ctx, client, newProjectId)
211: 	newHostSetId := boundary.CreateNewHostSetApi(t, ctx, client, newHostCatalogId)
212: 	newHostId := boundary.CreateNewHostApi(t, ctx, client, newHostCatalogId, c.TargetIp)
213: 	boundary.AddHostToHostSetApi(t, ctx, client, newHostSetId, newHostId)
214: 	newTargetId := boundary.CreateNewTargetApi(t, ctx, client, newProjectId, c.TargetPort)
215: 	boundary.AddHostSourceToTargetApi(t, ctx, client, newTargetId, newHostSetId)
216: 
217: 	acctName := "e2e-account"
218: 	newAcctId, _ := boundary.CreateNewAccountApi(t, ctx, client, acctName)
219: 	newUserId := boundary.CreateNewUserApi(t, ctx, client, "global")
220: 	uClient := users.NewClient(client)
221: 	uClient.SetAccounts(ctx, newUserId, 0, []string{newAcctId})
222: 
223: 	rClient := roles.NewClient(client)
224: 	newRoleResult, err := rClient.Create(ctx, newProjectId)
225: 	require.NoError(t, err)
226: 	newRoleId := newRoleResult.Item.Id
227: 	t.Logf("Create Role: %s", newRoleId)
228: 
229: 	_, err = rClient.AddGrants(ctx, newRoleId, 0, []string{"id=*;type=target;actions=authorize-session"},
230: 		roles.WithAutomaticVersioning(true),
231: 	)
232: 	require.NoError(t, err)
233: 
234: 	_, err = rClient.AddPrincipals(ctx, newRoleId, 0, []string{newUserId},
235: 		roles.WithAutomaticVersioning(true),
236: 	)
237: 	require.NoError(t, err)
238: }

(End of file - total 238 lines)
</content>
